search_queries_used: site:gov.uk diabetic eye screening pathway requirements result letters grading referral surveillance 2025
site:dha.gov.ae diabetic retinopathy screening guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic retinopathy or maculopathy grade interpretation
automatic surveillance interval or hospital-eye referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow cannot independently interpret a screening grade or image.
Referral and surveillance remain programme and clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People eligible for NHS diabetic eye screening; pregnancy, age, prior results and surveillance status change the pathway.
setting_applicability: Screening-result review and pathway handover, not a comprehensive ophthalmic examination.
UAE_applicability: The NHS pathway is international-only; UAE screening eligibility, grading, result communication and ophthalmology referral policy governs.
recency_verification: The NHS England pathway requirements updated 2025-04-16 were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current NHS England pathway requirements were identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NHS pathway section supports interval and patient-concern documentation plus review of previous endocrine or screening correspondence, without inferring eligibility or attendance.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording eligibility, attendance, prior pathway status, information sharing and coordination with primary care, diabetology, hospital eye and maternity services.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact screening-test and result section supports associated symptoms, relevant negatives, focused examination if actually assessed and clinician-reviewed result documentation without assigning a retinal grade.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording bilateral visual acuity, image status, grading, screening outcome and result letters sent to the person and relevant clinicians.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact surveillance and referral pathway supports recording a clinician-entered plan, safety discussion and arranged follow-up only when already determined; it does not generate referral or surveillance timing.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-confirmed surveillance, ungradable-image, digital-surveillance or hospital-eye-service referral status without autonomously assigning a grade or destination.

unresolved_source_gaps: Screening date, laterality, visual acuity, image quality, retinal and macular grade, result letter, pregnancy, surveillance status, interpretation, referral and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
